title: 17 Migrants Die, 9 Missing Off Libya After 8 Days Adrift
summary: At least 17 migrants died and nine remain missing after their boat broke down and drifted for eight days in the Mediterranean Sea off Libya.
contents:
## Source Context

OSINT collection from Twitter @detresfa_, anchored to a recent news report covering maritime distress, search and rescue operation, or migrant rescue in Mediterranean. This report surfaces developments aligned with the source's beat and gets logged here for downstream analysis.

## Event Summary

Libya’s Red Crescent, in coordination with naval units and coast guard forces of the Libyan National Army, rescued seven survivors during a recovery mission near Tobruk city, close to the Egyptian border. The vessel had broken down mid-route, leaving passengers without supplies for eight days. Rescue teams recovered 17 bodies after an eight-hour operation in challenging conditions. Security officials expect the nine missing migrants' bodies to wash ashore soon due to sea currents. Survivors received immediate medical attention and humanitarian support from the Red Crescent and UNHCR.

## Geographic Focus

Reporting points to Tobruk, eastern Libya.

## Topics and Actors

- Topics covered: migrant rescue, maritime distress, Mediterranean migration
- Actors named: Libya’s Red Crescent, Libyan National Army, Libyan coastguard

## Provenance

Primary citation comes from https://www.lokmattimes.com/international/libya-coast-17-migrants-killed-several-missing-after-vessel-breaks-down-in-mediterranean-sea-a525/; additional citations attached in the links array support cross-verification.
